title: herniaria incana subsp. besseri
intro: Herniaria incana subsp. besseri, also known as Bess's rampion or herniaria, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family that is native to Europe. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of herniaria incana subsp. besseri include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and treat urinary tract infections. Herniaria incana subsp. besseri can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves, roots, and flowers are used in various herbal preparations.
